doc.id: Potkay et al. - 2022 - Turgor-limited predictions of tree growth, height and metabolic scaling over tree lifespans 1.pdf
doc.title: Turgor-limited predictions of tree growth, height, and metabolic scaling over tree lifespans (Supporting Information)

model_card.name: Phloem transport model (PTM) — Notes S1 (C003)
task: compute phloem sap sucrose-equivalent concentration profile c_p(z̃) from axial flux j(z̃)
solve transformed ODE for dimensionless carbon potential Φ̃(z̃) with analytic integral term Θ(z̃)

assumptions.text: Sucrose is the only solute (confined to the phloem); “sugar” and “sucrose” are treated synonymously as a first-order approximation.

evidence.snippet: sucrose is the only solute and is confined to the phloem... first-order approximation...

assumptions.text: Sieve tube membranes are ideally semi-permeable to sucrose (reflection coefficient = 1).

evidence.snippet: ideally semi-permeable (reflection coefficient for sucrose is unity)...

assumptions.text: Molecular diffusion of sucrose is ignored because advection dominates transport by many orders of magnitude.

evidence.snippet: molecular diffusion of sucrose is ignored, since advection is the dominant mode...

assumptions.text: Sap viscosity μ is uniform along the stem (treated as a constant).

evidence.snippet: We assume viscosity, μ, is reasonably uniform... (μ is a constant)...

assumptions.text: Stem water potential gradient ψ′ is uniform (ψ′ constant).

evidence.snippet: assuming a uniform change in water potentials along the stem (i.e. ψ’ is a constant)...

assumptions.text: Xylem–phloem coupling yields near water-potential equilibrium: ψ_p = ψ_x = ψ.

evidence.snippet: xylem-phloem coupling enables water-potential equilibrium... (ψp = ψx = ψ)

assumptions.text: Phloem sap density is linear in sugar concentration: ρ_p = ρ_w + m_p c_p, with m_p fixed from a regression to 0.13 kg·mol−1.

evidence.snippet: ρp = ρw + mp cp (S.1.25)
we set mp equal to 0.13 kg·mol-1.

assumptions.text: Osmotic potential Π is linearized over expected c_p: Π = −(k_i + k_s ϖ R T c_p).

evidence.snippet: Π = −(ki + ks ϖ R T cp) (S.1.26)

assumptions.text: Square-root nonlinearity in Φ̃ is further linearized by a first-order Taylor approximation: √Φ̃ ≈ 1/2 + Φ̃/2, valid (≤10% error) for ~0.3 < Φ̃ < ~2.0.

evidence.snippet: reasonable as long as ~0.3 < Φ̃ < ~2.0 (less than 10% error)

assumptions.text: Phloem permeability varies as a power-law in z̃ and scales with height: κ_p = κ_p,0 (H/h)^λ z̃^δ.

equations.explanation: Turgor-driven advection (Münch-type) linking axial sucrose flux to turgor pressure gradient.

evidence.snippet: j = − cp κp/μ ∂P/∂z (S.1.22)

equations.explanation: Turgor gradient decomposed into water potential gradient, gravity, and osmotic potential gradient.

evidence.snippet: j = − cp κp/μ(ψ′ − ϖ ρp g − ∂Π/∂z) (S.1.23)

equations.explanation: Same advection relation in the dimensionless axial coordinate z̃ = z/H.

evidence.snippet: j = ... in our dimensionless coordinate system... (S.1.24)

equations.explanation: Linear density–concentration relationship for phloem sap.

evidence.snippet: ρp = ρw + mp cp (S.1.25)

equations.explanation: Linearized osmotic potential–concentration relationship; reduces to van’t Hoff when k_i=0 and k_s=1.

evidence.snippet: Π = −(ki + ks ϖ R T cp) (S.1.26)

equations.explanation: Carbon potential transformation and normalization by the apex value Φ_S = c_{p,S}^2/2.

equations.latex: -\frac{\mu j}{\kappa_p} = \sqrt{2\Phi_S}\,(\psi' - \varpi\rho_w g)\,\sqrt{\tilde{\Phi}} - 2\varpi g m_p\Phi_S\tilde{\Phi} + \frac{k_s\varpi R T\Phi_S}{H}\,\frac{\partial \tilde{\Phi}}{\partial \tilde{z}}
equations.explanation: Governing relation for Φ̃ after combining advection, density, and linearized osmotic potential; still nonlinear due to √Φ̃.
First-order Taylor approximation used to linearize the square-root term in Eq. S.1.28.
Power-law permeability profile capturing variation with tree size (H) and position (z̃).
Linear ODE for Φ̃(z̃) after applying Eq. S.1.29 and permeability scaling Eq. S.1.30.
Constant grouping water-potential gradient and gravity (via ρ_w).

evidence.snippet: θ = cp,S^2 (ψ′ − ϖ ρw g) (S.1.32)

equations.explanation: Constant grouping gravity, density–concentration slope, and apex carbon potential.
Constant grouping osmotic slope, temperature, and height scaling.
Resistance-like constant capturing permeability scaling with tree size.
Closed-form solution for Φ̃(z̃) given boundary condition Φ̃(0)=1.
Integral term appearing in the ODE solution; Θ has the same units as flux j.
Analytic Θ(z̃) obtained by substituting j(z̃) (Eq. S.1.21) into Eq. S.1.37; γ denotes the (lower) incomplete gamma function.

variables.name: phloem sap sugar concentration (sucrose equivalents)
variables.definition: Phloem sap sucrose-equivalent concentration along the stem.

evidence.snippet: phloem sap sugar concentrations... cp [(mol sucrose)·m-3]

variables.name: turgor pressure
variables.definition: Turgor pressure driving advection in sieve tubes.

evidence.snippet: P is turgor pressure [MPa]

variables.name: phloem permeability
variables.definition: Permeability of phloem conduits; κp·μ−1 is phloem conductivity.

evidence.snippet: κp is the phloem permeability [m4] ... (κp·μ−1 is the phloem conductivity)

variables.name: sap viscosity
variables.definition: Viscosity of phloem sap, assumed constant along the stem.

evidence.snippet: μ is the sap viscosity [MPa∙s] ... μ is a constant

variables.name: stem water potential gradient
variables.definition: Axial gradient of water potential along the stem (ψ′ = ∂ψ/∂z).

evidence.snippet: ψ’ is the stem water potential gradient [MPa∙m-1] (ψ’ = ∂ψ/∂z ...)

variables.name: Pa-to-MPa conversion factor
variables.definition: Conversion factor from Pa to MPa (ϖ = 10^-6 MPa·Pa^-1).

evidence.snippet: ϖ is a factor that converts from Pa to MPa (ϖ = 10-6 MPa ∙ Pa-1)

variables.name: phloem sap density
variables.definition: Density of phloem sap, modeled as a linear function of c_p.

evidence.snippet: ρp is the density of the phloem sap [kg∙m-3]

variables.name: water density
variables.definition: Density of pure water used in gravitational term and density model.

evidence.snippet: ρw is the density of water [kg∙m-3]

variables.name: acceleration due to gravity
variables.definition: Gravitational acceleration (g = 9.81 m·s^-2).

evidence.snippet: g is the acceleration due to gravity... (g = 9.81 m∙s-2)

variables.name: osmotic potential
variables.definition: Osmotic potential of phloem sap; linearized in c_p.

evidence.snippet: Π is the osmotic potential [MPa]

variables.name: density–concentration slope
variables.definition: Linear correction term relating sucrose concentration to solution density (set to 0.13 kg·mol^-1).

evidence.snippet: mp... units of molar mass... set mp equal to 0.13 kg·mol-1.

variables.name: osmotic linearization intercept
variables.definition: Intercept of linear regression between Π and ϖRTc_p.

evidence.snippet: ki is the intercept... [MPa]

variables.name: osmotic linearization slope
variables.definition: Slope of linear regression between Π and ϖRTc_p.

evidence.snippet: ks is the unitless slope [-]

variables.name: temperature
variables.definition: Temperature used in osmotic relation.

evidence.snippet: T is temperature [K]

variables.name: carbon potential
variables.definition: Transformed variable Φ = c_p^2/2 used to reduce nonlinearity.

evidence.snippet: carbon potential transformation, Φ ... Φ = cp^2/2

variables.name: dimensionless carbon potential
variables.definition: Φ̃ = Φ/Φ_S, where Φ_S = c_{p,S}^2/2.

evidence.snippet: normalize into a dimensionless form, Φ̃ [-]... ΦS = cp,S^2/2

variables.name: apex phloem concentration
variables.definition: Phloem sap concentration at the stem apex (z̃=0), used as normalization reference.

evidence.snippet: boundary condition... cp(z̃ = 0) = cp,S

variables.name: root-collar permeability at reference height
variables.definition: Permeability at the root collar (z̃=1) for a tree with reference height h.

evidence.snippet: κp,0 is the permeability at the root collar (z̃ = 1) for a tree with reference height, h

variables.name: reference height
variables.definition: Reference tree height used in permeability scaling.

evidence.snippet: reference height, h [m]

variables.name: height-scaling exponent for permeability
variables.definition: Exponent controlling permeability scaling with tree height H.

evidence.snippet: unitless scaling exponents, λ and δ, describe how permeability scales with tree size and distance...

variables.name: axial-scaling exponent for permeability
variables.definition: Exponent controlling permeability scaling with distance from the stem apex (in z̃).

evidence.snippet: λ and δ... distance from stem apex, respectively.

variables.name: ODE constant theta
variables.definition: Constant defined by Eq. S.1.32.

variables.name: ODE constant eta
variables.definition: Constant defined by Eq. S.1.33.

variables.name: ODE constant epsilon
variables.definition: Constant defined by Eq. S.1.34.

variables.name: ODE constant zeta (resistance-like)
variables.definition: Resistance-like constant defined by Eq. S.1.35.

variables.name: integral term in Φ̃ solution
variables.definition: Integral term (Eq. S.1.37) depending on j(z̃); has units of flux j.

evidence.snippet: Θ has units of j [(mol sucrose)·s-1].

parameters.name: m_p
parameters.estimation: Fixed from regression between solution density and sucrose concentration (Jensen et al., 2013).

evidence.snippet: we set mp equal to 0.13 kg·mol-1.
ϖ = 10-6 MPa ∙ Pa-1

parameters.name: kappa_p_0
parameters.estimation: Permeability at root collar for reference-height tree; specified elsewhere (Table 1 / Table S1).

parameters.name: lambda
parameters.estimation: Exponent for permeability scaling with height; specified elsewhere (Table 1 / Table S1).

parameters.name: delta
parameters.estimation: Exponent for permeability scaling with z̃; specified elsewhere (Table 1 / Table S1).

parameters.name: k_i
parameters.estimation: Linearized Π–(ϖRTc_p) intercept; specified elsewhere (Table 1 / Table S1).

evidence.snippet: ki is the intercept...

parameters.name: k_s
parameters.estimation: Linearized Π–(ϖRTc_p) slope; specified elsewhere (Table 1 / Table S1).

evidence.snippet: ks is the unitless slope

steps: Given j(z̃) from Eq. S.1.21 (C002), define κ_p(z̃) using Eq. S.1.30 and define constants θ, η, ε, ζ using Eqs. S.1.32–S.1.35.
Compute Θ(z̃) from Eq. S.1.38 (analytic form involving the incomplete gamma function) which is derived from Eq. S.1.37 and j(z̃).
Compute Φ̃(z̃) using the closed-form solution Eq. S.1.36 with boundary condition Φ̃(0)=1.
Recover concentration: c_p(z̃) = c_{p,S}\sqrt{\tilde{\Phi}(\tilde{z})} (implied by Eq. S.1.27).
Optionally compute ρ_p(z̃) via Eq. S.1.25 and Π(z̃) via Eq. S.1.26.

evidence.snippet: solution... boundary condition... Θ ... fully defined...

limitations.text: Assumptions of sucrose-only solute, ideal semi-permeability, and negligible diffusion simplify transport physics; deviations could affect inferred concentrations.

evidence.snippet: sucrose is the only solute... reflection coefficient... diffusion ignored...

limitations.text: Linearized osmotic potential Π(c_p) may miss nonlinear effects at large sugar concentrations; authors linearize over expected range.

evidence.snippet: nonlinear behaviors... influence... Hence, we apply... linearized...

limitations.text: Taylor approximation √Φ̃ ≈ 1/2 + Φ̃/2 is valid only within ~0.3 < Φ̃ < ~2.0 (≤10% error).

evidence.snippet: reasonable as long as ~0.3 < Φ̃ < ~2.0 (less than 10% error)

limitations.text: Permeability scaling exponents λ and δ bundle multiple allometric meanings (furcation and phloem area) and are not directly identical to previously reported within-branch conductance scalings.

evidence.snippet: λ and δ are not identical... meanings... represent furcation and phloem area...

implementation.model_scope: PTM subcomponent: concentration solution (S.1.3) using carbon potential transform and permeability power-law (Notes S1, pages 21–23).

evidence.snippet: ODE constants and closed-form solution for Φ̃.
Analytic Θ(z̃) from j(z̃) using incomplete gamma terms.

numerics.integration_method: analytic (closed-form Φ̃ and Θ; incomplete gamma functions)
numerics.stability_notes: Accuracy depends on Taylor approximation validity range for Φ̃.

evidence.snippet: ≤10% error range for √Φ̃ approximation.
boundary condition that Φ̃(z̃ = 0) = 1 (i.e. cp(z̃ = 0) = cp,S)

modules.name: ptm_concentration_solution
modules.responsibility: Compute κp(z̃), constants (θ,η,ε,ζ), Θ(z̃), Φ̃(z̃), and c_p(z̃) from j(z̃).

public_api.name: compute_kappa_p
public_api.signature: compute_kappa_p(z_tilde, H, h, kappa_p0, lambda_, delta) -> kappa_p

public_api.name: compute_constants
public_api.signature: compute_constants(cp_S, psi_prime, rho_w, varpi, g, m_p, k_s, R, T, H, mu, kappa_p0, h, lambda_) -> (theta, eta, epsilon, zeta)

public_api.name: compute_Theta
public_api.signature: compute_Theta(z_tilde, alpha, L_net, sigma, G, f_c, u_R, R_S, a, b, theta, eta, epsilon, delta) -> Theta

public_api.name: compute_Phi_tilde
public_api.signature: compute_Phi_tilde(z_tilde, theta, eta, epsilon, zeta, Theta) -> Phi_tilde

public_api.name: compute_cp
public_api.description: Implied inverse of Eq. S.1.27: cp = cp_S * sqrt(Phi_tilde).

provenance.notes: EXTRACT C003 (pages 21–23): PTM concentration solution (S.1.3). Defines turgor-driven advection, decomposes turgor components (water potential gradient, gravity via density, osmotic potential), applies carbon potential transform and Taylor linearization, introduces permeability power-law, derives linear ODE and closed-form Φ̃(z̃) with analytic Θ(z̃) involving incomplete gamma functions.

section_titles: S.1.3 Solution for phloem sap sugar concentrations
chunk_plan.rationale: Converts flux solution j(z̃) into concentration c_p(z̃) via carbon potential Φ̃, yielding an implementable closed-form solution and explicit parameter groupings (θ,η,ε,ζ) plus analytic Θ(z̃).

evidence.snippet: Defines Φ and Φ̃; Taylor approximation for √Φ̃.
Linear ODE, constants, boundary condition, closed-form Φ̃, and analytic Θ with incomplete gamma.
